cot_prefix: Okay, let's read through the article to find out which of the descriptions about Yves Gomes is true. First, the article starts by saying Yves Gomes is a university student in Maryland but may have to leave the country. He wants to continue his college education and go to medical school. This suggests he wants to stay in the US to become a doctor, which would rule out option (C) which says he wants to become a nurse in India. Next, the article says Gomes is one of thousands of young illegal immigrants who grew up in the US. He is fighting deportation to India, a country he left with his parents when he was 14 months old. His parents were deported more than a year ago. So he left India when he was 14 months old and came to the US. That would mean he was born in India, then brought to the US. So option (A) says he was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally. Wait, no, the article says he left India with his parents when he was 14 months old. That implies he was born in the US (since they left with him). Because if he was born in India, and his parents moved to the US when he was 14 months old, that would mean his parents moved with him, but the article says he is an illegal immigrant. So if he was born in the US, he'd be a citizen, but he is an illegal immigrant. So the fact that he is an illegal immigrant suggests he was born in India and brought to the US as an infant. But the article says he left India with his parents when he was 14 months old. So he was born in India, and then his parents moved with him to the US. So he was born in India, his parents are immigrants. But he is an illegal immigrant because they were not legal when they came. So option (A) says he was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally. Wait, but the parents would have immigrated before him. So if he left India with them when he was 14 months old, that suggests he was born in India. Therefore, option (A) is true? But wait, the phrasing of option (A) says "before his parents immigrated to America illegally." But if he left India with his parents, that means his parents were already in America when he was born? No, he was born in India, then left with his parents. So his parents immigrated to America illegally before he left India with them.
